mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when employment gains exceed 100k.
failure_condition: In periods where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side disruptions that increase inflationary pressures and reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by >5% but 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects robust economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% but 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates stagflationary risks that depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1% but 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor market conditions that push wages higher, increasing corporate costs and reducing equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 5% but 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved economic sentiment.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases by >5% but 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining consumer credit (RSAFS) reflects reduced household spending power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through weaker demand dynamics.
failure_condition: If RSAFS declines by >2% but 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
